items.title: Nieuwe spelregels aanstaande: VAR krijgt meer macht
items.summary: De International Football Association Board (IFAB) heeft meerdere spelregelwijzigingen aangekondigd die vanaf 1 juni van kracht worden. Dat betekent dat tijdens het komende WK verschillende nieuwe regels hun intrede doen.

items.title: VI Live: Heerlijk begin voor Liverpool, Van Dijk zorgt na 24 minuten voor 2-0
items.summary: Zaterdag belooft een fraaie voetbaldag te worden. Er staan drie potjes in de Eredivisie op het programma, waarvan die van PSV een eigen liveblog heeft. In het buitenland komen onder meer Liverpool, Manchester City en Barcelona in actie, terwijl in Duitsland Der Klassiker wordt gespeeld. Mis niets met VI Live.

items.title: Championship roundup: Norwich compound struggling Leicester’s home woes
items.summary: Leicester stay in drop zone after fourth straight home loss Hull match 60-year record with victory at Portsmouth Norwich condemned relegation-threatened Leicester to a fourth successive home Championship defeat to end Gary Rowett’s unbeaten start as manager. The second-half substitute Anis Ben Slimane drilled Norwich ahead just six minutes after coming off the bench at the King Power Stadium before Ali Ahmed’s close-range finish sealed a seventh win in nine league games. Continue reading...

items.title: Evanilson earns Bournemouth point as Sunderland dent Iraola’s Europe bid
items.summary: For those Sunderland fans making the 700-mile round trip from Wearside, some beginning their journey at midnight, the welcome sight of Granit Xhaka making a first start since 17 January. Could the captain’s return inspire Sunderland out of a slump that began in mid-December? On the south coast, a losing run came to its end as Régis Le Bris’s team got the point a dogged performance deserved. Patrolling in front of defence, Xhaka maintained constant dialogue with his colleagues, encouraging, directing into position, veering into the territory of player-manager. When Eliezer Mayenda scored the Sunderland goal, Xhaka clenched his fists in triumph, celebrating with Le Bris before heading to the away fans. For Bournemouth, it was a wholly preventable goal, Habib Diarra had been shown inside from Dan Ballard’s pass, and their goalkeeper Djordje Petrovic could only direct the ball into Mayenda’s path. Continue reading...

items.title: Inschattingsfout Roefs kost Sunderland twee punten aan Engelse zuidkust
items.summary: De Premier League-wedstrijd tussen Bournemouth en Sunderland is zaterdagmiddag in een gelijkspel geëindigd. Robin Roefs maakte lange tijd indruk, maar leidde uiteindelijk puntenverlies in voor de bezoekers.

items.title: FIFA to 'monitor developments' in Iran ahead of WC
items.summary: FIFA will "monitor developments" in Iran following the outbreak of military action by the United States -- the co-hosts of this summer's men's World Cup -- against the Middle Eastern nation.

items.title: Madrid-City again in UCL draw, Chelsea get PSG
items.summary: Real Madrid have been handed another mouthwatering clash with Manchester City in the round-of-16 draw for the Champions League, while holders Paris Saint-Germain face Chelsea.

items.title: Messi knocked to ground as fans invade field in PR
items.summary: Lionel Messi was knocked to the ground by a fan and a security guard at the end of Inter Miami's friendly match in Puerto Rico on Thursday.

items.title: FIFA weighs mouth-covering rule after Vini Jr case
items.summary: Consultations will be held about measures to combat players covering their mouth when confronting opponents during matches, it was announced on Saturday.

items.title: More VAR power, more countdowns for World Cup
items.summary: VAR's powers have been extended by football's lawmakers ahead of the 2026 World Cup.

items.title: PSG granted more time off between Chelsea ties
items.summary: Paris St-Germain will have five days off between the two legs of their Champions League last-16 tie against Chelsea after their request to postpone a Ligue 1 match is granted.

items.title: VAR to review second yellows and corners as timewasting clamped down on
items.summary: VAR's powers have been extended by football's lawmakers.

items.title: Hoe houdt Van Persie rekening met mentale gezondheid spelers?
items.summary: Het betaald voetbal vraagt dit weekend aandacht voor mentale gezondheid, een onderbelicht probleem in de maatschappij en ook in het voetbal. Feyenoord-trainer Robin van Persie bespreekt het thema.

Damsgaard heads Brentford in front at Burnley LIVE!
Wider VAR scope and more countdowns to tackle time-wasting at World Cup
items.summary: Corners and second yellow cards are added to the scope of the video assistant referee for this summer's World Cup.

items.title: Bijzonder moment: scheidsrechter geeft penalty terwijl bal over achterlijn was
items.summary: Er vond vrijdagavond een bijzonder moment plaats tijdens het duel Jong FC Utrecht - Almere City, toen scheidsrechter Clay Ruperti besloot een penalty te geven. Dat deed hij in de wetenschap dat de verdediger de bal verplaatste met zijn hand om de doeltrap te nemen en de bal niet uit was geweest, terwijl op beelden te zien is dat het wel het geval was.

items.title: West Ham must sell players in summer after £104m loss
items.summary: West Ham will need to sell players this summer even if they avoid relegation from the Premier League, after revealing a £104.2m loss for the last financial year.

items.title: Arsenal are easy targets for TikTok mockery – they must not be prisoners of that narrative | David Hytner
items.summary: Goaded by Tottenham and lampooned by Wolves, Mikel Arteta’s side face Chelsea this weekend with rivals hoping to prey on their sensitivities Arsenal had to expect the jibes from Tottenham and they were not disappointed. Just before kick-off in last Sunday’s north London derby, the Spurs support in the South Stand of their stadium spelled out a giant message in mosaic form. “North Ldn since 1882,” it read. A clear dig, then, at Arsenal’s south of the river history from the Woolwich days and, as mentioned, very much a part of a rivalry where goading and baiting go hand in hand with the loathing. Arsenal had been similarly provocative before the derby against Spurs at the Emirates Stadium last November, lighting the pre-match scene with a tifo featuring images of various club greats. The most prominent at the top of it? Sol Campbell, of course. Continue reading...
